{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query is about the normal growth of the maxilla. Document [5] mentions that the maxilla typically grows anteriorly, inferiorly, and laterally. This growth is described as a combination of processes including concurrent resorption of bone from the anterior surface of the ramus with apposition of bone along the posterior surface of the mandible. This process, in conjunction with growth at the condyle, creates the anterior and inferior vector of growth. This description aligns with the process of apposition, which is the addition of new bone tissue to the surface of existing bone. Therefore, the normal growth of the maxilla occurs by apposition.", "answer_choice": "C"}
